title: Reasoning, Planning, Decomposition, and Verification · Prompt Lens: Uncertainty Report
summary: Prompt a difficult migration without exposing or demanding hidden reasoning. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt uncertainty report increment in a plan-execute-verify task contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Require bounded uncertainty, missing evidence, and blockers to remain visible.
Ask for targeted final checks tied to known failure modes instead of vague self-critique.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Uncertainty Report
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt reasoning planning. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt reasoning planning. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Uncertainty Report contract.

steps.title: Build Uncertainty Report evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-uncertainty-report-1c41f9" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Uncertainty Report into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-uncertainty-report-1c41f9
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

stimulus.title: Uncertainty Report failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

steps.title: Defend the Uncertainty Report model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Uncertainty Report applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Require bounded uncertainty, missing evidence, and blockers to remain visible.
